items.title: Brighton v Bournemouth: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Updates on the 8pm (GMT) kick-off at the Amex ⚽ Live scores | Top scorers | Read Football Daily It’s one of football’s more bittersweet accolades: the unofficial title of the Premier League’s greatest selling club. Sure, it means you’re an excellently run club, unlike most of your peers, and have an enviable scouting network. But it also means you have to constantly regenerate – Bournemouth have inevitably had a tough season – and endure the low-level frustration of watching players you discovered become superstars elsewhere. In the last year Bournemouth have taken that unofficial title from Brighton, selling five major players for around £250m. The two teams meet at the Amex Stadium tonight, and there will almost certainly be some superstars of the future in action. Continue reading...

items.title: Jonathans op zoek naar zelfvertrouwen: 'Het kan nog steeds lukken in Utrecht'
items.summary: Miliano Jonathans (21) stond bij Vitesse te boek als een groot talent en maakte een mooie transfer naar FC Utrecht, maar daar kwam hij nog niet bijzonder veel aan spelen toe. De aanvaller heeft met een verhuur aan Excelsior nu bewust een stapje teruggezet.

items.title: Transfers PSV: 'Tai Abed vertrekt naar Spanje'
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij PSV op de voet, zeker nu de winterse transferwindow in aantocht is. De doelwitten van de Eindhovenaren, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: Ajax en Willem II zijn het eens over transfer Deens talent Abildgaard
items.summary: Lasse Abildgaard heeft een doorbraak bij Ajax, in ieder geval voor nu, uit zijn hoofd gezet. De Deense aanvallende middenvelder maakt de overstap naar Willem II.

items.title: Wintertransfers Eredivisie seizoen 2025/26: alle clubs op een rij
items.summary: De transferperiode is weer losgebarsten. In dit overzicht van VI vind je alle afgeronde, door clubs bevestigde, wintertransfers van de achttien Eredivisie-clubs. Clubs in Nederland kunnen tot en met 2 februari spelers aantrekken .

items.title: 'Het is niet De Treffers tegen Jasper Cillessen'
items.summary: Jasper Cillessen maakt zich op voor een bijzondere wedstrijd in het toernooi om de KNVB Beker. De doelman gaat dinsdag met NEC op bezoek bij De Treffers, zijn jeugdclub uit zijn woonplaats Groesbeek. 'Mensen die me kennen, weten dat ik altijd wil winnen. Dus ook tegen De Treffers.'

items.title: Under-pressure Frank 'feeling trust' of Spurs board
items.summary: Tottenham head coach Thomas Frank says lunch with board members is an "extremely good sign" despite pressure mounting on him during an underwhelming season.

items.title: Conte makes subtle dig at Amorim over Højlund
items.summary: Napoli head coach Antonio Conte has appeared to aim a dig at former Manchester United head coach Ruben Amorim.

items.title: ‘The dream will have to wait’: Moroccan media reacts to Afcon final heartache
items.summary: Walid Regragui faced calls to resign despite stunning record with Atlas Lions after nightmare that prolongs 50-year wait “There will be no second star for Morocco,” read the headline of Le360.ma, referring to the symbol of an Africa Cup of Nations title being sewn on the shirt of the winning team. “The most prestigious African title is at odds with Morocco. The dream and the ambition will have to wait some time,” laments the writer, Abdelkader El-Aine. It is now 50 years since Morocco won their only Afcon. This month, as hosts of the tournament, Morocco had started to dream of a second title. That became a nightmare on Sunday evening in Rabat. Continue reading...

items.title: Which managers might be free in the summer?
items.summary: A summer of managerial change could be on the cards with a World Cup, contracts expiring, and clubs already hunting for new bosses.

items.title: Frank feels 'trust' from Spurs board and admits he can't win over all fans
items.summary: Tottenham boss Thomas Frank has admitted that he won't be able to win over every Spurs fan after being booed during the home defeat to West Ham on Saturday.

items.title: City confirm $26.8M Guehi signing from Palace
items.summary: Manchester City have announced the signing of England centre-back Marc Guéhi from Crystal Palace on a five-and-a-half-year deal.

items.title: FIFA chief Infantino: AFCON chaos unacceptable
items.summary: Senegal leaving the field to protest referee decisions before winning the Africa Cup of Nations title amid shocking chaos was "unacceptable," FIFA president Gianni Infantino said on Monday.

items.title: McKenna: 'Hard to be consistent' in incredibly close Championship
items.summary: A slow-ish start to the season held back a squad many considered to be the best in the Championship, but the improvement at Ipswich Town has been ominous in recent weeks.

items.title: A miracle could not save them - but an NFL giant is back
items.summary: Cigar-crowned Mike Ditka outfits. A snow-glittered Soldier Field. A Caleb Williams miracle. Chicago Bears bedlam. Chicago Bears heartbreak. It was emotion-strangling NFL playoff football at its quintessential box office best.

items.title: Djokovic's Australian Open century sends message in bid for 25th Grand Slam
items.summary: Novak Djokovic notched his 100th Australian Open win in his 2026 tournament opener on Monday, but reaching a quarter century of Grand Slam titles remains the number of more interest to him.

items.title: Real Sociedad steal a march on Barcelona to get city party started early | Sid Lowe
items.summary: Carnival drums provided the backdrop to surprise victory over league leaders that was not just down to luck “There was a little magic in the atmosphere,” Pellegrino Matarazzo said. Real Sociedad’s new coach could feel it; he could hear it too, the sound of drums beating on every street of the city he has embraced and into the stadium that has embraced him back already. When he and his players arrived at Anoeta on Sunday evening, they entered through a guard of honour, a band of soldiers and chefs lined up in the rain, hammering out the club anthem and hoping. By the time they departed around midnight, following 35,346 supporters out into San Sebastián, it had actually happened. La Real had beaten Barcelona 2-1. Celebrations, his captain Mikel Oyarzabal said, had come a day early. This week is tamborrada, the San Sebastián festival where, at midnight on 20 January, the city flag is raised and marching bands parade through its streets in Napoleonic uniforms and cooks’ costumes grasping sticks, batons and giant cutlery, routes mapped out in loving detail and special supplements. Initially it was a popular pastiche of a military procession, a prelude to carnival, practice runs echoing round in the days before, kids go first, adults next. An expression of civic pride, they sing of “spreading joy,” being “always happy,” and God knows they were happy now. What better way to begin it all than this? What better way to become one of them? Continue reading...

items.title: Man City sign Guehi from Palace - what will he bring?
items.summary: Man City sign Crystal Palace captain Marc Guehi for £20m - but what will the England defender add to Pep Guardiola's side?

items.title: Marc Guéhi completes £20m move to Manchester City from Crystal Palace
items.summary: England defender’s contract was due to expire in summer City are without injured Dias, Gvardiol and Stones Marc Guéhi has completed his £20m move to Manchester City from Crystal Palace. The England international has signed a contract to 2031 after choosing the club over other offers. An agreement was reached on Friday and the England defender was withdrawn from Palace’s weekend game at Sunderland to finalise the transfer. Guéhi was close to joining Liverpool last summer before Palace pulled the plug but the Premier League champions decided not to revive their interest because they saw no value in a January deal for a player out of contract in the summer. Continue reading...

items.title: Guehi completes Man City move: 'I am now at the best club in England'
items.summary: Manchester City have completed the signing of defender Marc Guehi from Crystal Palace in a deal worth an initial £20m.
